show.title: The Phantom of the Opera (2004)
show.headline: The classic musical comes to life on the big screen

event.title: <event-type-title>Alamo Crafthouse:</event-type-title> <show-title>The Phantom of the Opera (2004)</show-title>
event.headline: Craft in the shadows of a classic romance
event.description: How’s this for a strange duet? Joel Schumacher’s 2004 gothic romance paired with hands-on crafting designed just for the movie. Each guest receives two craft sets to work on from their seat, including Phantom-inspired calligraphy with guided activity sheets and a card to fill in, sign, and seal. You’ll also create an iconic red paper rose with step-by-step instructions, all while enjoying food and drinks delivered right to your seat.

contentBlocks.paragraphs: How’s this for a strange duet? Joel Schumacher’s 2004 gothic romance paired with hands-on crafting designed just for the movie. Each guest receives two craft sets to work on from their seat, including Phantom-inspired calligraphy with guided activity sheets and a card to fill in, sign, and seal. You’ll also create an iconic red paper rose with step-by-step instructions, all while enjoying food and drinks delivered right to your seat.

eventType.title: Alamo Crafthouse
eventType.headline: Enjoy your favorite movies with a crafting activity tailored for the film
eventType.description: At Alamo Crafthouse, we're bringing your favorite movies with a bit of extra fun. Each event includes an activity tailored specifically for the film, from crocheting to calligraphy, and the lights will stay on so you can craft along to the movie. Let's sip, snack, and craft!

show.title: Toy Story 5
show.headline: Something new has entered the playroom

event.title: <event-type-title>Alamo Crafthouse:</event-type-title> <show-title>Toy Story 5</show-title>
event.headline: Make your own toy right from your seat
event.description: Choose from Forky or Karen Beverly when you enter the theater and let our costumed host guide you through the process of bringing them to life. Each craft kit comes pre-packaged with everything needed to bring your new plasticware friend to life without needing a screen to follow along. It’s part movie, part craft time, and all about remembering that play doesn’t always need to be digital.

event.title: <show-title>jackass: best and last</show-title> <event-type-title>Cereal Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: The most important meal of the day + the most important movie ever made
event.description: Self-inflicted pain goes down easier with a bottomless bowl of Cap’n Crunch, and our Cereal Party screenings of JACKASS: BEST AND LAST pair the most irresponsible stunts imaginable with an all-you-can-eat buffet of childhood breakfast favorites and your choice of milk. Grab a spoon, grab your buddies, and start your day off in the dumbest way possible.

eventType.title: Cereal Party
eventType.headline: Awesome movies. Unlimited cereal. ‘Nuff said.
eventType.description: Belly up to an all-you-can-eat cereal bar. We’ll provide bowls, spoons, and a big selection of cereal to crunch on throughout the film. Pajamas encouraged.

titleCardImage.altText: Cereal Party TT

show.title: Minions & Monsters
show.headline: Lights! Camera! Banana!

event.title: <show-title>Minions & Monsters</show-title> <event-type-title>Family Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Bring your little minions
event.description: Turn your screening into a full-on Hollywood disaster zone with our interactive Family Party screenings, the perfect way to experience MINIONS & MONSTERS with kids. A live host will guide families through the mayhem with themed keepsakes including Minion goggles, monster slime, squishy bananas, and a clapboard keychain, plus a make-at-your-seat craft activity during the show. It’s silly, hands-on fun built for little movie Minions and the grown-up Grus trying to keep up with them.

show.title: Field of Dreams
show.headline: If you believe the impossible, the incredible can come true

event.title: <show-title>Field of Dreams</show-title> <event-type-title>+ His Own Way: The Eddie Russell Story</event-type-title>
event.eventTypeTitleOverride: + His Own Way: The Eddie Russell Story
event.headline: Step into the cornfield, celebrate the bourbon
event.description:
Step up to the plate and reserve a spot at our free screening of FIELD OF DREAMS paired with the short documentary HIS OWN WAY: THE EDDIE RUSSELL STORY, tracing Wild Turkey Master Distiller Eddie Russell’s journey from his earliest days at the distillery to his lasting influence on American whiskey today. The experience includes branded Russell’s Reserve merchandise and a themed menu featuring Ball Park Mac & Cheese and Shake Me Out to the Ballgame peanut butter shake, plus Russell’s Reserve bourbon cocktails like Field of Blackberry Dreams and Eddie's Old Fashioned. Reserve a spot with a refundable food and non-alcoholic beverage voucher, redeemable at the event. There won’t be a dry eye or an empty glass in the house.

Note: This event is for ages 21+.

eventType.title: Free Victory Screening
eventType.headline: Our way of saying thank you to our Victory Members
eventType.description: Calling all Victory Members! We’re saying thanks with a free screening. Just buy a fully refundable food & beverage voucher to reserve your seat, and then use it toward food and drink at the show.

show.headline: How far would you go to sleep with Jon Hamm?

event.title: <show-title>Gail Daughtry and the Celebrity Sex Pass</show-title><event-type-title> with Livestream Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Your sex pass arrive early with a livestream Q&A
event.description: Ever considered who your celebrity pass would be? Before you start drafting paperwork, join us for a special advance screening of David Wain's wildly misguided Hollywood odyssey, followed by a livestream Q&A with the director and Zoey Deutch. Find out who their sex passes would be and how they convinced Jon Hamm to play himself.

show.title: Maddie's Secret
show.headline: Make way for melodrama in John Early's bold debut

event.title: <show-title>Maddie's Secret</show-title> <event-type-title>w/Livestream Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Meet Maddie early with a John Early livestream Q&A
event.description: Be among the first to see MADDIE'S SECRET at our special advance screening featuring a livestream Q&A with writer, director, and star John Early, moderated by Emmy winner Hannah Einbinder (Hacks). Before the conversation begins, dig into an exclusive themed menu inspired by Maddie's vegetarian lifestyle and the film's recurring hidden pineapple motif, including the Hidden Pineapple Veggie Sandwich, GourMaybe Eggplant Pizza, and specialty cocktails Going Viral and Ride Or Die. It's the perfect way to immerse yourself in one of the funniest, strangest, and most original movies of the year.

show.title: 13 Going on 30
show.headline: For some, 13 feels like it was just yesterday. For Jenna, it was.

event.title: <show-title>13 Going on 30</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Celebrate being 30, flirty & thriving at our interactive movie party
event.description:
When 13 GOING ON 30 was released over 20 years ago(!), I was overjoyed at the fact that Hollywood had finally decided to make BIG for girls. Don't get me wrong, it was awesome to watch Tom Hanks spend his paycheck on video games and a penthouse with a trampoline, but now it was a gal's turn to live the dream, and Jennifer Garner did not disappoint. This film features the greatest fantasies of a 13-year-old girl*: a makeover montage in a gigantic closet, a high-power job working at a fashion magazine, a massive slumber party, and, of course, a totally cute guy.

But 13 GOING ON 30 isn't just a fun premise, it's a heartfelt and hilarious coming-of-age story. The cast is top-notch, with Garner perfectly embodying her inner teen, Judy Greer serving up some major sass (like she does), and Mark Ruffalo being as foxy and charming as he's ever been. This delightful cinematic concoction is better than a pack of Razzles, so join us and experience the thrill of being 30, flirty, and thriving.

*Or any girl, regardless of her age. Because that shoe collection! WANT. (Sarah Pitre)

Your ticket includes the following props:

  • Butterfly tattoo – Just like Jenna's favorite accessory.
  • Tissues – No need to look like a model. You're beautiful just as you are.
  • Mantra sleepmask – The best years of your life.
  • Wishing dust - What do you wish for in your heart of hearts?

show.title: But I'm a Cheerleader
show.headline: A comedy of sexual disorientation

event.title: <show-title>But I'm a Cheerleader</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Conversion camp has never been this fun
event.description: Grab your pom-poms and report to Harmony, because we’re throwing a live-hosted movie party for BUT I’M A CHEERLEADER. You’ll be decked out with themed props, including an orange and white pom-pom, a blue necktie, a hair clip, a Pride flag, and a heart carabiner as you follow Megan’s journey from suburban confusion to “I kissed a girl and I liked it” realisation. This is one movie party where there’s no reason to learn to be normal.

show.title: Elf
show.headline: The best way to spread Christmas cheer

event.title: <show-title>Elf</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: A cheerful celebration with themed games, props & singing loud for all to hear
event.description:
"Buddy the Elf, what's your favorite color?"

Don’t be a cotton-headed ninnymuggins this holiday season. Come out to Alamo Drafthouse and spread the cheer at this sugar-fueled, interactive holiday spectacular, packed with games, props, and, of course, singing loud for all to hear.

Bundle up for this celebration of one of the best holiday films ever made, period. Will Ferrell’s take on a North Pole elf trying to figure out Manhattan (“Watch out! The yellow ones don’t stop!”) just might be the funniest he’s ever been.

Our ELF Movie Party features all sorts of fun props, a gift "wrap off" before the film, and of course, your very own elf hat.

show.title: Jurassic Park
show.headline: Spielberg's rampaging dinosaur masterpiece proves life finds a way

event.title: <show-title>Jurassic Park</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Life finds a way to party
event.description:
Hold onto your butts, it’s the epic interactive screening 65 million years in the making!

John Hammond was a man with a simple dream of satisfying his lawyers and insurance agents so that he could open a park where humans would once again be free to walk the Earth with massive creatures that enjoy killing them. From that dream, a new legend was hatched (under BD Wong’s close observation, of course) – our JURASSIC PARK Movie Party. So get in your self-driving electric Jeep and pay attention to the rippling water in that plastic cup as you join us for a cinematic experience unlike any other!

We spared no expense for Steven Spielberg’s all-time classic. Check the boxes in the back of your Jeeps (aka your table) for glow sticks, hand soap, a car magnet, a ladle, dino poop, and everything else you need for a fun-filled time with some two-story-tall genetic monstrosities. After all, what could possibly go wrong?

show.title: The Fast and the Furious: 25th Anniversary
show.headline: Start your engines

event.title: <show-title>The Fast and the Furious: 25th Anniversary</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: A NOS-fueled party for family
event.description: Join us for a high-octane movie party celebrating the 25th anniversary of the original FAST movie. Before the cars in Tokyo drifted, before they went to space, and before any Vin Diesel feuds, it was just a simple undercover cop story about stolen VCRs, street racing, and loyalty tested at 100 miles per hour. Strap in for this special screening with a live host, exclusive themed keepsakes like a bumper magnet, bottle opener, and a NOS keychain, as well as a theater full of family.

show.headline: Every record has two sides

show.title: Six: The Musical Live!
show.headline: Witness the hit West End musical about Henry VIII's six wives in theaters

event.title: <show-title>Six: The Musical Live!</show-title> <event-type-title>Sing-Along</event-type-title>
event.headline: Belt out the hits from the West End smash
event.description:
Get ready to lose your head at this special sing-along screening of SIX: THE MUSICAL LIVE! This is your chance to rule the room alongside the queens and sing along with every iconic number in a theater full of fellow Tudorphiles.

Please note that our usual No Talking/No Singing Showtunes policy is relaxed for this screening, but please be reasonable and save a little vocal power for the rest of the monarchy.

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Cannibal Holocaust
show.headline: The mother of all cannibal films

event.title: <show-title>Cannibal Holocaust</show-title>
event.headline: The mother of all cannibal films, newly restored.
event.description:
Originally released in 1980, Ruggero Deodato's controversial horror classic remains one of the most infamous and influential films in the genre. Now remastered in 4K, see this gruesome classic like it's never been seen before.

CANNIBAL HOLOCAUST presents the "found footage" of four documentary filmmakers who experience brutal death at the hands of a savage South American tribe of flesh-eaters. The footage is so intense, so graphic, and so unflinching in its realism that the director and producer of CANNIBAL HOLOCAUST were arrested on its original release and the film was seized. Predating THE BLAIR WITCH PROJECT in its use of "found footage," CANNIBAL HOLOCAUST also served as a key inspiration for Eli Roth’s THE GREEN INFERNO.

Be forewarned: This is the one that goes ALL THE WAY!

show.title: Bob & Carol & Ted & Alice
show.headline: Bob and Carol are ready to embrace complete openness in their relationship

event.title: <show-title>Bob & Carol & Ted & Alice</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Olivia Wilde ahead of her new film THE INVITE
event.description:
BOB & CAROL & TED & ALICE is presented as part of our Olivia Wilde Guest Selects for her new film, THE INVITE.

Paul Mazursky’s 1969 film is completely of its moment – the encounter groups, the therapy-speak, the newly minted vocabulary of openness. American film had never talked about marriage and polyamory this way before, with this mix of cynicism and warmth, self-awareness and romance. What it understands best is friendship – inside a marriage and outside it. Mazursky lets his four leads be ridiculous and sincere in the same breath, and never condescends to any of them. The Vegas ending, scored to Bacharach’s “What the World Needs Now,” is one of the strangest, most disarming swerves in American comedy. (Olivia Wilde)

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Female Trouble
show.headline: A high point in low taste

event.title: <show-title>Female Trouble</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by John Early ahead of his new film MADDIE'S SECRET
event.description:
FEMALE TROUBLE is presented as part of our John Early Guest Selects for his new film, MADDIE'S SECRET.

FEMALE TROUBLE was kind of the birth of MADDIE'S SECRET, because before I had any sort of narrative, I just had an ethos that I wanted to replicate, and it was FEMALE TROUBLE. It was the handmade quality of FEMALE TROUBLE, the guerrilla-style filmmaking, the way John Waters was working with his friends. That's what I wanted to do with MADDIE'S SECRET – I wanted to make something that was as propulsive as FEMALE TROUBLE. It's the movie that made me. It was the first John Waters movie I ever saw, and it totally changed my life. I wept uncontrollably when I saw it for the first time – it was mostly just because I was like, "Well, I've always wanted to do that." I've always, for whatever reason, wanted to be the lead actress. I sat around and waited for the John Waters to my Divine to come along, and y'all, he never showed up. And so I had to make MADDIE'S SECRET and be both things. (John Early)

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Force Majeure
show.headline: Ruben Östlund's dark comedy about a family vacation disrupted by an avalanche

event.title: <show-title>Force Majeure</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Olivia Wilde ahead of her new film THE INVITE
event.description:
FORCE MAJEURE is presented as part of our Olivia Wilde Guest Selects for her new film, THE INVITE.

Ruben Östlund, the master of excruciating and hilarious cynicism, creates a devastating marital crisis out of two seconds of cowardice on a family ski trip. The brilliance of this film to me is the juxtaposition of chaos and control: man-made explosions reshaping landscapes, politely, in hushed hotel hallways. Östlund shoots in long, locked-off takes that put the performances center stage, letting the tension build with no escape from the frame. The thunderous Vivaldi score acts as a relentless Greek chorus, reminding us of the existential calamity occurring behind tight smiles of domestic desperation. Somehow, this is both the funniest and most devastating movie I’ve ever seen. (Olivia Wilde)

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Imitation of Life (1959)
show.headline: Fannie Hurst's best-selling novel of a tormented generation

event.title: <show-title>Imitation of Life (1959)</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by John Early ahead of his new film MADDIE'S SECRET
event.description:
IMITATION OF LIFE (1959) is presented as part of our John Early Guest Selects for his new film, MADDIE'S SECRET.

IMITATION OF LIFE is my favorite Douglas Sirk movie. It doesn't get much better. It's a total shocking masterpiece. If you try to make something that's a melodrama, people inevitably think you're in conversation with Sirk. It's almost like the whole point of MADDIE'S SECRET was I wanted to see what it was like for the kind of cadences, the way people spoke, the kind of expressiveness of the acting, and the filmmaking of these Sirk movies – what would happen if you superimposed that onto contemporary life? I thought it could be potentially very funny to hear people say things like, "Hulu password," as if they were Lana Turner or Sandra Dee in this movie.

If you don't know anything about Douglas Sirk, these were women's pictures that were, at their time, in some ways popular, but they were critically dismissed as being soapy or melodramatic. Then they were reevaluated and now are seen as great works of art, as they should be. The moment that I think of all the time: Lana Turner plays an actress who's having this fight with her daughter, Sandra Dee, and Turner goes into this little monologue. She's tearing up and looking right on the corner of the matte box. It's like she's almost making eye contact with the audience. She gives this beautiful speech, and then it cuts to Dee, and she goes, "Oh, stop acting, Mother." And it's a perfect little meta, ironic moment that feels like it was engineered for gay people to laugh at. (John Early)

show.title: The Brady Bunch Movie
show.headline: They're back to save America from the '90s!

event.title: <show-title>The Brady Bunch Movie</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by John Early ahead of his new film MADDIE'S SECRET
event.description:
THE BRADY BUNCH MOVIE is presented as part of our John Early Guest Selects for his new film, MADDIE'S SECRET.

This movie, in some ways, created my sense of humor. I watched this movie obsessively at a very young age. I think I am equal parts Christine Taylor and Jennifer Elise Cox in my comic DNA. If I'm being honest, the reason why I wanted to make MADDIE'S SECRET is because I wanted to do something like what Christine Taylor does in The Brady Bunch movies, plural. Her performance of Marcia Brady is so angelic and precise, and she's this beautiful blonde who everyone worships or is sexually obsessed with – and for some reason, I wanted to do that. THE BRADY BUNCH MOVIE is perfect, sharp satire while also having a giant heart. I would say that is what makes it most connected to MADDIE'S SECRET – there is a kind of edge and a rage, even, or a kind of cultural commentary going on, but it also has a bleeding heart. It's very sincere, and it's ultimately, I think, a very positive movie, despite some of its subject matter. (John Early)

show.title: Johnny Guitar
show.headline: One of the most daring and subversive westerns that Hollywood ever produced

event.title: <show-title>Johnny Guitar</show-title>
event.headline: Queer icon Joan Crawford butches it up in this influential Nicolas Ray western
event.description:
“I’ve never met a woman who was more man.”

Joan. Crawford. Ms. No Wire Hangers herself. A queer icon. A legendary pair of eyebrows. The Ultimate Movie Star. In choosing which Crawford film to screen as part of Queer Film Theory 101, we really could’ve gone in any direction. We decided to go with a somewhat lesser-known yet highly influential gem from director Nicholas Ray, JOHNNY GUITAR from 1954.

When reformed gunslinger Johnny Guitar (Sterling Hayden) returns to the small Arizona cattle town he once resided in, he’s met with a complicated reception from his former lover, saloon owner Vienna (Joan Crawford). The drama escalates as Emma Small (Mercedes McCambridge), Vienna’s rival, targets her and her establishment for associating with local gang leader “Dancin' Kid” (Scott Brady), the man responsible for killing her brother. Now don’t get it twisted, the film may be called JOHHNY GUITAR, but the real action of this stylized western isn’t focused on the titular Mr. Guitar at all; instead, the true story and so much of the film's queerness comes from the tense and sexually charged relationship between its female leads, Crawford and McCambridge. These two women (and actresses for that matter) hold a bitter and violent resentment of each other, but at the same time, so clearly and desperately want to have gay sex. These two tough cookies can't take their eyes off each other, and every scene they share oozes with hatred and desire. Mix all of this with Crawford’s exceptionally butch styling and dommy mommy charisma, and you’ve got a potently queer-coded film perfect for Queer Film Theory 101. See you out on the range, cowgirls!

show.headline: The husband, the wife...or the bandit?

show.title: Tiny Furniture
show.headline: Lena Dunham explores an authentic portrait of a young woman at a crossroads

event.title: <show-title>Tiny Furniture</show-title>
event.headline: Lena Dunham’s awkwardly charming debut reflects her longstanding career
event.description:
Although a polarizing figure, Lena Dunham has created some of the most quintessential millennial coming-of-age films of the 2010s. Her breakout, oddball SXSW indie TINY FURNITURE was the blueprint for her hit TV series Girls, and also starred Jemima Kirke and Alex Karpovsky.

In the film, Dunham plays a version of herself: Aura, an aspiring film graduate who returns to live with her mom and sister in New York after feeling the independence of college. Aura is ready for her career to begin, but doesn’t know where to begin, and takes up a restaurant gig to make money while she sorts out her goals.

Dunham’s projects have always felt personal, but there’s a certain rawness to TINY FURNITURE that’s uncomfortably disturbing but almost impossible to look away from the trainwreck. In the wake of her new memoir Famesick, it’s nostalgic to go back to see where it all began.

show.title: The Faculty
show.headline: What if your teachers really were from another planet?

event.title: <show-title>The Faculty</show-title>
event.headline: THE BREAKFAST CLUB meets INVASION OF THE BODY SNATCHERS
event.description: What do you get when you combine the distinct visual eye of Robert Rodriguez with the wit of SCREAM scribe Kevin Williamson? You get THE FACULTY, a late '90s, fun-as-hell entry in sci-fi canon that sees a group of teenagers team up against their body-snatched teachers when aliens decide to land at the doorstep of their high school. Featuring an all-star cast of fresh-faced actors (Elijah Wood, Josh Hartnett, Jordana Brewster, Clea DuVall) as the students and seasoned vets (Bebe Neuwirth, Famke Janssen, Robert Patrick, Piper Laurie) as the titular academics, THE FACULTY is a merging of talents so perfect that no '90s audience could have possibly been prepared. So grab a nice, tall glass of water and get ready for a movie that's guaranteed to jack you up! (Trace Thurman)

show.title: Buffet Infinity

event.title: <show-title>Buffet Infinity</show-title> <event-type-title>+ MAN EATING P***Y Double Feature</event-type-title>
event.headline: A double feature of oddball comedies
event.description:
About BUFFET INFINITY
2026 / 99 mins.

Echoing the Canadian comedy classic SCTV and picking from hundreds of hours of original, low-budget TV ads to tell the sinister tale of two restaurants battling it out in the fictional town of Westridge County. Ads for insurance, used car rivals, a local religious scholar, and a recording artist converge to tell the story of an expanding sinkhole, a cult, and an ever-growing restaurant that becomes unsettlingly sentient.

About MAN EATING P****Y
2026 / 15 mins

A terminally ill man finds solace with an enigmatic prostitute whose inhuman body provides both carnal satisfaction and final deliverance.

show.title: Howard The Duck
show.headline: Not bad for a duck from outer space...

event.title: <show-title>Howard The Duck</show-title>
event.headline: Not bad for a duck from outer space...
event.description:
Back in 1986, Lucasfilm and Universal thought it would be a good idea to bring an anthropomorphic duck named Howard to screen in live-action glory. He’s a private eye! He talks! He carries condoms made for ducks! Somehow, the powers that be thought there was a recipe for success here...and boy were they right.

HOWARD THE DUCK is typically regarded as a flop and a completely misguided attempt at giving us a talking duck movie. But it’s bigger than that. HOWARD THE DUCK straddles the line between careful adaptation and a reimagining of a property as something decidedly different (and perhaps much more adult) than its source material would suggest.

It’s not every day that you get a studio-produced comic book movie from the director of MESSIAH OF EVIL, the creator of STAR WARS, and features a cigar-chomping duck who thinks he’s Sam Spade and has a lust for human women. Cherish it. It’ll never happen again.

show.title: Nowhere
show.headline: Sex, drugs, and alien abductions: just another day of high school

event.title: <show-title>Nowhere</show-title>
event.headline: Sex, drugs, and alien abductions: Just another day of high school
event.description: The 4K restoration of Gregg Araki’s NOWHERE is a stylized foray into the nightmare world of adolescent highs and lows, but more than that, it functions as a study of an entire generation and the direction they’re headed. Where THE DOOM GENERATION seemed to be a wake-up call, NOWHERE is more of a reflexive inquiry into the destiny of the seemingly apathetic youth who place more emphasis on style than substance. An ensemble piece with James Duval (star of all three of the films in the Teen Apocalypse Trilogy), NOWHERE features a cast that mixes well-known faces — Ryan Philippe, Christina Applegate, Debi Mazar, Heather Graham, Guillermo Diaz, Denise Richards, Beverly D’Angelo, Shannen Doherty, and Tracy Lords — with up-and-comers. Restored and remastered, Araki’s final version includes scenes omitted from the original release due to rating restrictions. NOWHERE is a sexy and wild ride, so buckle up, remain seated, and enjoy the ultimate fast trip to oblivion. (Strand Releasing)

show.title: The Devil Queen
show.headline: A lurid, fabulous dive into Brazil's criminal underworld

event.title: <show-title>The Devil Queen</show-title>
event.headline: A lurid, fabulous dive into Brazil's criminal underworld
event.description: Rio de Janeiro’s criminal underworld is run by an unexpected boss: Diaba (Milton Gonçalves), a femme queen with a taste for power and violence. When the police come after her boy toy, Diaba attempts to find a scapegoat to take the blame and a plot to dethrone Her Majesty arises! Always clad in gloriously colorful outfits and accessories inspired by Afro-Brazilian culture, Diaba runs a mob of eccentric misfits made up of drag queens, pimps, prostitutes, and queer folks of all stripes. The movie’s bold and audacious color palette and camp aesthetic evokes the early films of Pedro Almodóvar, while its stylized and over-the-top violence is giving Quentin Tarantino queer fever dream.

An Alamo Drafthouse Private Event presented by Pickford AI. To book your premiere, visit drafthouse.com/private-events.

Pickford presents an interactive double feature event. Whispers is an AI-powered whodunnit like you've never seen before. Audience members will "whisper" in the ears of Detective Marcus Kent to help crack the case and identify the killer in real-time.

Opening for Whispers is First Date, a comedic interactive experience where the audience creates our dating heroes, coaches what they say, and determines whether romantic sparks fly or ensures they crash and burn.

show.title: Talladega Nights: The Ballad of Ricky Bobby
show.headline: The story of a man who could only count to 1

event.title: <show-title>Talladega Nights: The Ballad of Ricky Bobby</show-title> <event-type-title>20th Anniversary Re-Release</event-type-title>
event.headline: An action comedy based in the world of professional stock car racing
event.description: NASCAR stock car racing sensation Ricky Bobby (Will Ferrell) is a national hero because of his "win at all costs" approach. He and his loyal racing partner, childhood friend Cal Naughton Jr. (John C. Reilly), are a fearless duo – "Thunder" and "Lightning" by their fans for their ability to finish so many races in the #1 and #2 positions, with Cal always in second place. When flamboyant French Formula One driver Jean Girard (Sacha Baron Cohen) challenges "Thunder" and "Lightning" for the supremacy of NASCAR, Ricky Bobby must face his own demons and fight Girard for the right to be known as racing's top driver.

marketStrings.value: The personal closed captioning device displays feature film dialogue and other audio in the form of English-language text for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

The descriptive audio headset provides individuals who are blind or have low vision with an English-language narration of the details of the feature presentation that can't be understood by the soundtrack alone.

Upon request at the box office, we provide assistive listening headsets with amplified audio for those individuals who are hard of hearing. For guests who prefer to use their own hearing aids, neck loops, or cochlear implants, our assistive listening headsets have a 3.5mm headphone jack to connect to those devices.

In order to ensure the highest quality experience for all guests, you may not enter a movie after it has started. We’d love to see you up to 30 minutes early. Running late? No worries, we will happily exchange your ticket for a different showtime or a raincheck to a future show.

sessionAttributes.name: All Ages
sessionAttributes.description: For all ages shows, we welcome babies and kids of all ages. The no talking rules are relaxed, since kids will be kids. But other than that, you can expect the full Alamo experience.

sessionAttributes.name: Baby Day Show
sessionAttributes.description: For our baby day shows, we keep the lights up a bit, and the sound down a bit, so your little one never has to know they even went to the movies!

sessionAttributes.name: Kid Friendly
sessionAttributes.description: Grab the fam and let the fun begin! Our Kid Friendly screenings include pre-show activity sheets and crayons, a relaxed noise policy, and kids menus. Ages 3+ welcome with a parent or guardian.

formats.title: 2D Digital
formats.headline: Digital format projected using a digital projector.
formats.description: Digital format projected using a digital projector.

formats.title: 3D Digital
formats.headline: All the digitalness of the Digital 2D format, but with an extra dimension!
formats.description: All the digitalness of the Digital 2D format, but with an extra dimension!

formats.title: 35mm
formats.headline: Film print projected using film projectors.
formats.description: The industry standard for theatrical film releases prior to digital cinema. Film print projected using film projectors.

agePolicies.name: All Ages
agePolicies.description: All minors under the age of 18 must be accompanied by an adult. Children ages 6+ admitted. Children 5 and under (including infants) are ONLY admitted during specifically designated "All Ages" and "Family Friendly" screenings.
